title: 10 best herbal tinctures for plantar fasciitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for plantar fasciitis are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, prepared by soaking plant material in a solvent such as alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures can provide relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and tissue-healing properties. Common herbal tinctures used for plantar fasciitis include turmeric, ginger, willow bark, and devil's claw. For instance, turmeric contains curcumin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation, while ginger has natural pain-relieving abilities. Willow bark and devil's claw are also known for their anti-inflammatory effects. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into a treatment plan, individuals may experience reduced pain, inflammation, and faster healing, ultimately improving their mobility and overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. The herb's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, work together to improve blood flow, alleviate tension, and promote tissue repair. By enhancing the body's natural healing process, yarrow tincture can offer relief from the symptoms of plantar fasciitis, allowing individuals to maintain their daily activities with increased comfort and mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture helps reduce inflammation in the foot, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can alleviate pain and promote healing of the inflamed tissue. The anti-inflammatory properties come from compounds such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture has pain-relieving properties that can help manage the discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis. This is important because it can improve mobility and overall quality of life. The analgesic effects are attributed to compounds like alkaloids and flavonoids.

Astringent: Yarrow tincture can help reduce excess tissue fluid and promote tissue contraction, which is beneficial for plantar fasciitis as it may help strengthen the affected tissue. The astringent properties are due to tannins present in yarrow.

Antispasmodic: Yarrow tincture can help alleviate muscle spasms and tension, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can provide relief to the tight calf muscles and plantar fascia. The antispasmodic effects are attributed to lactones and essential oils found in yarrow.

Circulation-enhancing: Yarrow tincture can improve blood flow and circulation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can promote healing and reduce inflammation. The circulation-enhancing properties are due to the volatile oils and flavonoids present in yarrow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, or 1 ounce of dried yarrow, and place in a clean, dry jar.
Fill the jar with 8 ounces of 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol, ensuring that all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the finished yarrow tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for plantar fasciitis pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica flower tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the heel and foot. Arnica has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce swelling, making it an effective treatment for plantar fasciitis. The herb's natural pain-relieving qualities can also help alleviate the discomfort associated with this condition. Additionally, arnica tincture can be easily applied topically, allowing for targeted relief in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and providing pain relief, arnica flower tincture can help promote healing and improve mobility for those suffering from plantar fasciitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Arnica flower tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it alleviates pain and swelling caused by the inflammation of the plantar fascia. The main active constituents responsible for this property are sesquiterpene lactones, such as helenalin and its derivatives.

Analgesic: Arnica flower tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it helps manage the sharp or dull pain in the heel and arch. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are the same sesquiterpene lactones, including helenalin.

Circulatory stimulant: Arnica flower tincture improves blood flow, which is essential for plantar fasciitis as increased circulation promotes healing and reduces inflammation in the affected area. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils, such as thymol and carvacrol.

Antispasmodic: Arnica flower tincture helps relieve muscle spasms and tension, which is beneficial for plantar fasciitis as it can alleviate the tightness and discomfort in the foot muscles. The active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and tannins.

Tissue repair promotion: Arnica flower tincture supports tissue repair and regeneration, which is vital for plantar fasciitis as it aids in healing the damaged plantar fascia. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential fatty acids, plant sterols, and polysaccharides.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh arnica flowers, gently rinse and let dry for 12 hours.
Chop flowers and place in a clean jar, add 10 ounces of 80 proof vodka, seal and store in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks.
Shake the jar daily and strain the mixture after 2 weeks, squeezing out the liquid from the flowers.
Store the strained liquid in a clean, amber glass bottle and label with the ingredients and date.
Use 1-2 dropperfuls of the tincture on the affected area of plantar fasciitis, 3 times a day, until symptoms improve.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Daisy tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. The herb's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and luteolin, have been shown to alleviate symptoms of plantar fasciitis by promoting tissue repair and reducing swelling. Additionally, daisy tincture may improve blood flow to the feet, which can help accelerate the healing process and provide relief from the stabbing heel pain associated with this condition. By incorporating daisy tincture into a well-rounded treatment plan, individuals with plantar fasciitis may experience improved mobility and a better quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Daisy tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the plantar fascia, providing relief from pain and swelling, due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids.

Analgesic: The analgesic property of daisy tincture helps alleviate pain associated with plantar fasciitis, as it contains active constituents such as pyrethrins and tannins that interfere with pain signaling.

Antispasmodic: Daisy tincture's antispasmodic property helps relax the muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can relieve tension in the plantar fascia and improve overall foot function, thanks to the presence of alkaloids and saponins.

Circulatory stimulant: The circulatory stimulant property of daisy tincture improves blood flow to the feet, promoting healing and reducing inflammation, due to the presence of volatile oils and glycosides.
Tissue repair promotion: Daisy tincture supports tissue repair and regeneration, which can help heal damaged plantar fascia tissue, due to the presence of vitamins, minerals, and other nutrients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh daisy flowers, filling a clean jar twice with tightly packed herb, then cover with 80-proof vodka, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Securely seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place, shaking daily for two to four weeks to encourage extraction.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent plant material and retaining the liquid.
Using a funnel, transfer the liquid to a dark glass dropper bottle, labeling it with the contents and date.
Take 20-30 drops of the daisy tincture three times daily, mixing it with a small amount of water or directly under the tongue, for plantar fasciitis relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and inflammation in the affected area. The herb contains flavonoids, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. Additionally, pot marigold tincture may improve blood flow to the feet, which can help speed up the healing process and provide relief from the symptoms of plantar fasciitis. Its soothing and calming effects on the nervous system may also help reduce muscle tension and promote relaxation, further aiding in the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in the foot, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it is characterized by inflammation of the plantar fascia. Active constituents include flavonoids and triterpenes.

Analgesic: It alleviates pain, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it can cause significant discomfort and pain in the heel and arch of the foot. Active constituents include salicylic acid and volatile oils.

Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of microorganisms, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can sometimes lead to secondary infections. Active constituents include thiophenes and flavonoids.

Circulation-boosting: Pot marigold tincture improves blood flow, which is important for plantar fasciitis as increased circulation can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Active constituents include saponins and essential oils.

Tissue-repair: It promotes tissue repair and regeneration, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it can cause micro-tears in the plantar fascia. Active constituents include carotenoids and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean jar two-thirds full.
Combine 1 cup of vodka or grain alcohol and 1 cup of filtered water in a separate container.
Pour the alcohol-water mixture over the flowers until completely submerged, stirring gently.
Cover the jar with a tight-fitting lid and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it is known for its anti-inflammatory and circulation-boosting properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation and pain in the plantar fascia, the thick band of tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot and can become inflamed and irritated in people with plantar fasciitis. Additionally, gotu kola has been shown to improve collagen formation and strengthen connective tissue, which can help repair and prevent further damage to the plantar fascia. The tincture form of gotu kola is also easily absorbed by the body, allowing for quick and effective relief of plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Gotu kola tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the plantar fascia, relieving pain and promoting healing. This is due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid.

Wound healing: Gotu kola tincture promotes wound healing, which is essential for repairing damaged plantar fascia tissue. The active constituents responsible for this property include triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which stimulate collagen synthesis and improve blood flow.

Analgesic: Gotu kola tincture has analgesic properties that help alleviate pain associated with plantar fasciitis. The presence of triterpenoid saponins and essential oils, such as eugenol, contribute to its pain-relieving effects.

Antioxidant: Gotu kola tincture's antioxidant properties help protect plantar fascia tissue from oxidative stress and damage. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which scavenge free radicals and reduce oxidative stress.

Venotonic: Gotu kola tincture has venotonic properties that improve venous insufficiency and microcirculation, which is beneficial for plantar fasciitis. The active constituents responsible for this property include triterpenoid saponins, such as asiaticoside and madecassoside, which strengthen capillary walls and improve blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried gotu kola herb, place in a clean glass jar, then pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the herb to cover it completely.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
After the desired steeping time, strain the liquid using cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle.
Discard the leftover herb and tightly seal the bottle.

Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, ingredients, and date of preparation, then store in a cool, dark place for up to 2 years. Suggested dosage for plantar fasciitis: 30-60 drops, 3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and pain in the heel and foot. This makes it an effective natural remedy for plantar fasciitis, a common condition characterized by pain and stiffness in the plantar fascia, the band of tissue that runs along the bottom of the foot. The tincture's concentrated form allows for faster and more efficient absorption of curcumin, providing quicker relief from symptoms. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected area, further aiding in the recovery process.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its curcuminoids like curcumin, helps reduce inflammation in the plantar fascia, providing pain relief for plantar fasciitis.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of turmeric tincture, caused by its curcuminoids, alleviates pain associated with plantar fasciitis, improving patient comfort.
Antioxidant: Turmeric tincture's high antioxidant content, primarily from curcumin, helps protect the plantar fascia from oxidative stress and supports tissue repair.
Fibrinolytic: The fibrinolytic activity of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcumin, aids in breaking down fibrin, reducing scar tissue formation, and promoting healing in plantar fasciitis.

Immune modulation: Turmeric tincture's ability to modulate the immune system, facilitated by curcumin, can help regulate the body's inflammatory response, supporting the healing process in plantar fasciitis.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh turmeric root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring the turmeric is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the finished turmeric tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use as needed for plantar fasciitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild yam tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. The herb is rich in steroidal saponins, such as diosgenin, which is believed to have a similar structure to cortisone, a hormone that helps reduce inflammation. Additionally, wild yam tincture may help improve circulation and relax muscles, which can further alleviate the symptoms of plantar fasciitis. Its natural anti-spasmodic properties can also help relieve muscle tension and cramping, providing additional relief to individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild yam tincture helps reduce inflammation in the foot, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can alleviate pain and swelling in the affected area. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the presence of steroidal saponins such as diosgenin.

Analgesic: Wild yam tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it can help manage the sharp pain that often accompanies this condition. The analgesic effects are due to the presence of isoflavones and steroidal saponins.

Antispasmodic: Wild yam tincture can help relax muscles and alleviate muscle spasms, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can reduce tension in the plantar fascia and calf muscles. The antispasmodic properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and steroidal saponins.

Circulation-enhancing: Wild yam tincture can improve blood flow and circulation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can promote healing and reduce inflammation. The circulation-enhancing effects are due to the presence of steroidal saponins and essential oils.

Immune-boosting: Wild yam tincture can help strengthen the immune system, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it can support the body's natural healing processes. The immune-boosting properties are attributed to the presence of polysaccharides and steroidal saponins.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of wild yam root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 quart of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the chopped wild yam root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 dropperfuls (20-40 drops) up to three times a day for plantar fasciitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can alleviate pain and inflammation in the affected area. The herb's active compounds, such as harpagoside, have been shown to reduce swelling and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for plantar fasciitis. Additionally, devil's claw may improve mobility and flexibility in the feet, allowing individuals to resume their normal activities with greater ease. Its potent healing properties can help speed up the recovery process and provide long-term relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Devil's claw tincture can reduce inflammation, which is important for plantar fasciitis as it is characterized by inflammation of the plantar fascia. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the iridoid glycosides, particularly harpagoside.

Analgesic: Devil's claw tincture can alleviate pain, which is crucial for plantar fasciitis as it can cause significant heel and foot pain. The analgesic effects are due to the harpagoside and other iridoid glycosides.

Improves mobility: Devil's claw tincture can enhance mobility and reduce stiffness, which is beneficial for plantar fasciitis as it can restrict movement. This is attributed to the anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Antioxidant: Devil's claw tincture has antioxidant properties, which are important for plantar fasciitis as they can help reduce oxidative stress and promote healing. The antioxidant activity is due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Immune-modulating: Devil's claw tincture can help regulate the immune response, which is significant for plantar fasciitis as it involves an overactive immune response. The immune-modulating effects are attributed to the iridoid glycosides and other phytochemicals.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried devil's claw root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use 1-2 mL up to three times daily for plantar fasciitis pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The herb contains several active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. When applied topically, the tincture can help soothe inflamed tissue in the foot, thereby easing the pain and discomfort associated with plantar fasciitis. Furthermore, St. John's wort has been found to improve microcirculation, which can enhance the healing process and promote the regeneration of damaged tissue. As a result, the tincture can provide effective relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms while supporting the body's natural healing abilities.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the plantar fascia, thereby alleviating pain and promoting healing. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Analgesic: St. John's wort tincture has pain-relieving properties, which can help manage the pain associated with plantar fasciitis. Hypericin and hyperforin are the primary active constituents that contribute to this medicinal property.

Antispasmodic: St. John's wort tincture can help relax muscles and alleviate spasms, which can be beneficial for plantar fasciitis as it can reduce tension in the foot muscles and plantar fascia. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and various alkaloids.

Circulation-enhancing: St. John's wort tincture can improve blood flow and circulation, which can help promote healing in the plantar fascia. The active constituents responsible for this property include hyperforin and essential oils such as sesquiterpene alcohols and monoterpenes.

Antimicrobial: St. John's wort tincture has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections in any wounds or cuts on the feet, reducing the risk of complications in individuals with plantar fasciitis. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully open and dry, typically in mid-summer.
Chop or crush 2 ounces of the herb, placing it in a clean, sterilized glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged, then seal the jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, discarding the plant material, and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Recommended dosage is 30 drops, 3 times a day, applied topically to the affected area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with plantar fasciitis because it contains anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the symptoms of this condition. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to reduce inflammation in the affected area, which can help to relieve pain and discomfort. Additionally, lavender has been shown to improve circulation and promote relaxation, which can further aid in the healing process. By using a lavender tincture, individuals with plantar fasciitis may experience reduced pain and inflammation, allowing them to resume their normal activities with greater ease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the plantar fascia, providing relief from pain and swelling associated with plantar fasciitis. This property is attributed to the presence of linalyl acetate and linalool.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of English lavender tincture helps alleviate pain caused by plantar fasciitis. This is due to the presence of linalool and lavandulol.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic property of English lavender tincture helps relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms in the feet, which can provide relief from plantar fasciitis symptoms. This property is attributed to the presence of linalool, lavandulol, and caryophyllene.

Circulatory stimulant: English lavender tincture's circulatory stimulant property helps improve blood flow to the feet, which can aid in healing and reducing inflammation associated with plantar fasciitis. This property is attributed to the presence of 1,8-cineole.

Anxiolytic: The anxiolytic effect of English lavender tincture can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can help improve sleep and overall well-being, promoting faster healing and recovery from plantar fasciitis. This property is attributed to the presence of linalool and linalyl acetate.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh English lavender flowers, filling a clean, dry mason jar halfway, ensuring proper storage in a cool, dark place for up to two weeks.
Combine the lavender with 80-proof vodka, filling the jar to 1 inch below the rim, then securely fastening the lid for eight weeks while stored in a cool, dark location.
Shake the jar gently every few days to ensure even extraction of the herbal properties.
After eight weeks, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean, dry jar or bottle, discarding the spent lavender flowers.
Label the final tincture with the ingredients, preparation date, and recommended dosage (typically 20-30 drops, three times a day) for plantar fasciitis symptom relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat plantar fasciitis with herbal tinctures is by using anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving herbs. For instance, a tincture made from turmeric or ginger root can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Additionally, a tincture containing St. John's Wort has been shown to help with nerve pain. Take these tinctures orally, as directed on the label. Moreover, a tincture made from arnica or calendula can be applied topically to the affected area to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Remember to always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to plantar fasciitis that are treated with herbal tinctures include inflammation of the tendons and ligaments, as well as muscle strains and sprains. Herbs such as willow bark, turmeric, and ginger are often used in tincture form to reduce inflammation and manage pain. Arnica, another commonly used herb, is known for its ability to alleviate muscle and joint pain, making it a popular choice for individuals experiencing discomfort in the feet and heels. Additionally, calendula tincture is sometimes used to promote tissue healing and reduce swelling. As with any treatment, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal regimen.
